title: GMS Week 19 - DOWN AND DIRE!
summary:
Main
It has been a woeful week across all of the major ship recycling markets, with some tumultuous declines leading to minimal interest and offers on any available tonnage. It increasingly seems as though it may be a much softer summer given prevailing sentiments, especially as prices ease back towards the (still impressive) USD 650s/LDT mark. Most of the deals concluded at USD 700/LDT (or above) were always seen as precariously positioned, and it remains to be seen whether End Buyers will be happy to dip back into the buying at these comparatively lower levels, given the global state of depreciating currencies and declining steel fundamentals.
